supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on suppliers for testing equipment, supplies and other goods and services. Inflationary pressures have resulted in increases in costs of testing equipment, supplies and other goods and services purchased from manufacturers, suppliers and others.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions including shortages, delays and price increases in testing equipment and supplies. Suppliers and manufacturers may experience disruptions and delays stemming from raw material and labor shortages, supply challenges and significant disruptions in transport and logistics services due to facility closures, labor constraints and other challenges.

regulatory_risks: FDA regulation of laboratory developed tests (LDTs) - removal of enforcement discretion requiring compliance with Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act over four-year process

Clinical Laboratory Improvement Act (CLIA) certification requirements and potential violations resulting in monetary fines, criminal and civil penalties, suspension or exclusion from Medicare/Medicaid programs
Medicare and Medicaid billing compliance - anti-kickback laws and anti-self-referral laws prohibiting payments to influence referrals
PAMA (Protecting Access to Medicare Act) reimbursement rate reductions - potential 15% annual cuts from 2026-2028
State clinical laboratory licensing requirements with varying personnel qualifications, quality control, record maintenance and proficiency testing requirements
Privacy and security regulations including HIPAA, state laws (California), European Union GDPR, Canada's Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
Environmental, health and safety regulations related to handling, transportation and disposal of medical specimens, infectious and hazardous waste and radioactive materials
Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) workplace safety requirements for healthcare employers
Drug Enforcement Administration licensing requirements for controlled substances used in drugs-of-abuse testing
Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration certification for drug testing for public sector and federally regulated employees
Corporate practice of medicine restrictions in certain jurisdictions prohibiting business corporations from engaging in practice of medicine
Physician licensing requirements in jurisdictions where medical services are provided
Federal and state interoperability requirements for healthcare data
Price transparency requirements including good faith estimates for self-pay patients and disclosure of cost information to consumers
Potential changes in healthcare regulation following 2024 Presidential election and new administration
Federal government budget pressures and potential changes in budgetary priorities affecting Medicare and other government programs
